page: Microsoft Azure E128-32ads_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Eadsv7 server family. It is built on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and features 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor. The instance is equipped with 1024.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per vCPU.

For local data persistence, the server includes 7559 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. It does not feature any graphics processing units, with a GPU count of zero. This configuration prioritizes massive memory capacity and local storage space over accelerated parallel computing resources.

From a resource density perspective, this virtual machine offers high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring substantial memory footprints relative to compute cores. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes it suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and data analytics tasks that do not require GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure E128-32ads_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the Eadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and is equipped with 1024.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per vCPU. Storage capabilities include 7559 GB of local storage, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. There are no onboard GPUs. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads, offering high resource density for applications requiring massive RAM allocations relative to compute capacity. The server is suited for large in-memory databases, heavy enterprise applications, and data processing tasks that demand substantial local storage and memory capacity without the need for hardware-accelerated graphics.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and extensive local storage for demanding in-memory database workloads.
